title: Stylogyne chartacea
intro: Stylogyne chartacea, also known as " Chartacea Root", is a plant species belonging to the family Primulaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it a potential remedy for various health issues such as wound healing, fever reduction, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Stylogyne chartacea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Stylogyne chartacea is commonly used in traditional medicine and can be found in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in some modern pharmaceutical products.
taxonomy: Stylogyne chartacea is classified within the family Primulaceae, which includes a group of flowering plants commonly known as the primrose or the primulid family. Stylogyne chartacea is further classified within the subfamily Myrsinoideae, a subfamily within the Primulaceae family. This plant is a part of the genus Stylogyne, which is primarily found in tropical regions.
